name: Banarasi Silk Saree — Crimson & Gold
description: An exquisite handwoven Banarasi silk saree featuring intricate gold zari work on a deep crimson base. The brocade pattern showcases traditional Mughal-inspired floral motifs passed down through six generations of master weavers in Varanasi.

name: Kanjivaram Silk — Royal Purple
description: A magnificent Kanjivaram silk saree in regal purple with a wide gold temple border. Each saree from this family workshop in Kanchipuram takes over 30 days to weave, using pure mulberry silk and real gold-dipped zari threads.

name: Chanderi Silk Cotton — Ivory Bloom
description: A delicate Chanderi silk-cotton saree in soft ivory adorned with intricate gold butti patterns. Known for its sheer texture and lightweight drape, this fabric is handwoven by artisans who have perfected the Chanderi technique for centuries.

name: Pochampally Ikat — Indigo Geometry
description: A striking Pochampally Ikat saree featuring bold geometric diamond patterns in deep indigo and white. Created using the resist-dyeing technique where yarns are tied and dyed before weaving, making each piece a unique work of art.

name: Pashmina Shawl — Natural Heritage
description: A luxurious hand-embroidered Pashmina shawl in natural cream, featuring intricate Sozni needlework with paisley motifs in muted earth tones. Made from the finest Changthangi goat wool, this shawl represents months of painstaking craftsmanship.

name: Jamdani Muslin — Ethereal White
description: An ethereal Jamdani muslin saree in soft white with delicate hand-woven floral and geometric motifs. This ancient textile art form, recognized by UNESCO, requires extraordinary skill as patterns are woven directly into the fabric on the loom.

name: Khadi Cotton — Natural Earth
description: A beautifully textured handspun Khadi cotton fabric in natural undyed cream. Each meter of this cloth is hand-spun on a charkha and handwoven, embodying the spirit of self-reliance and sustainable fashion that Khadi represents.

name: Patola Silk — Double Ikat Heritage
description: A masterpiece of the double ikat technique from Patan, Gujarat. The Patola saree features intricate geometric patterns with elephants and flowers in vibrant red, green, and gold. Only a handful of families worldwide preserve this 900-year-old craft.
